text.title: caching-stampede-prevention Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for caching-stampede-prevention as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using SETNX then EXPIRE as two separate commands for distributed lock acquisition.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If the process crashes after SETNX but before EXPIRE, the lock key has no TTL and the lock is held indefinitely — permanent deadlock. The atomic SET NX EX command eliminates this race.

failure-mode.description: Deleting the lock key without checking ownership (simple DEL instead of Lua check-and-delete).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If the lock TTL expires while the lock holder is still loading data, another process reacquires the lock. When the original holder finishes and calls DEL, it silently releases the new owner's lock, allowing a second concurrent rebuild — defeating the entire stampede guard.

failure-mode.description: No fallback when a lock acquisition fails: returning an error response instead of serving stale data or falling through to origin.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A cache layer that returns errors during the stampede window amplifies the incident instead of degrading gracefully. Always serve stale or fall through to origin as a last resort.
